compiled_state.description:
## Why
The pattern only pays off if future sessions can discover and reuse it. A first-class doc is
how the next agent adds an ensure correctly, understands the invariants, and knows the
accepted trade-offs and the A-tier future.

## What
Write `docs/migrations.md` AND add a link to it from the docs index and CLAUDE.md. Cover: how
to add an ensure unit (append to the `ensures.py` registry with a stable id + a check-then-act
callable returning ok/changed/failed); the entry points (init/re-init, `rebar fsck --repair`,
MCP server startup — all reconcile unconditionally under the write lock); the applied-set
`.ensure-applied` marker + the `.ensure-hinted` rate-limited pending-hint; the
marker-gates-hint-never-repair invariant; the hot-path performance contract (no sweep on
writes; ≤1 marker read/process); the ACCEPTED long-lived-MCP trade-off (converges at boot,
does not re-sweep mid-life — drift caught at next `fsck --repair`); and the A-tier future
(committed store-format version + ordered ledger, fail-closed on too-new) with the
committed-version recommendation. Use gc-config as the worked reference unit.

## Scope
In: `docs/migrations.md` + the index/CLAUDE.md link. Out: code (WS1-3), tests (WS5).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `docs/migrations.md` documents adding an ensure unit (registry + stable id + check-then-act + typed result) with gc-config as the worked reference
- [ ] Documents the entry points, `.ensure-applied` + `.ensure-hinted`, the pending-hint, and the marker-gates-hint-never-repair invariant
- [ ] Documents the hot-path performance contract AND the accepted long-lived-MCP re-sweep trade-off
- [ ] Documents the post-rollback behavior (old code ignores the git-ignored markers; committed `.gitattributes`/`.gitignore` content persists harmlessly)
- [ ] Captures A-tier future (committed store-format version + ordered ledger, fail-closed on too-new) + committed-version recommendation
- [ ] A link to `docs/migrations.md` is added from the docs index and CLAUDE.md (verifiable: the link string is present in both)
compiled_state.title: WS4: docs/migrations.md — first-class ensure-pattern reuse guide

verify_commands.command: test -f docs/migrations.md && grep -q marker-gates docs/migrations.md && grep -rq migrations.md CLAUDE.md
